title: EIP-8116: Replace cumulative receipt fields
description: Replaces the cumulative `cumulativeGasUsed` field in on-chain receipts with a per-transaction `gasUsed` value. Also changes the JSON-RPC `logIndex` from a block-level index to a per-receipt index. These changes remove stateful dependencies between receipts, enabling more efficient verification and greater parallelism in receipt processing.
author: Etan Kissling (@etan-status), Gajinder Singh (@g11tech)
laymanDescription: Changes transaction receipts so each one only records its own gas usage instead of a running total for the whole block. Also makes log numbering per-transaction instead of per-block. This simplifies verification and allows more work to happen in parallel.

endUsers.description: No direct impact. Receipt data changes are transparent to end users interacting with wallets and applications.

appDevs.description: Applications parsing `cumulativeGasUsed` or block-level `logIndex` from receipts must update to use per-transaction equivalents.

walletDevs.description: Wallets displaying gas usage from receipts may need minor adjustments if they consume raw receipt fields directly.

toolingInfra.description: Block explorers, indexers, and analytics tools must update receipt parsing to handle per-transaction `gasUsed` and `logIndex` semantics.

layer2s.description: Rollups deriving data from L1 receipts must adapt to the new per-transaction fields when verifying or re-executing transactions.

stakersNodes.description: No direct impact beyond running updated client software that produces the new receipt format.

elClients.description: Moderate implementation work: receipt construction must switch from cumulative to per-transaction fields, and JSON-RPC log indexing logic must change.

clClients.description: Minimal impact. Consensus clients do not directly construct or interpret execution receipt fields beyond fork-activation plumbing.

benefits: Enables parallel receipt processing by removing inter-receipt state dependencies.
Simplifies RPC client verification of individual transaction gas usage.
Aligns on-chain receipt data with how applications actually consume it.
Reduces computational overhead for indexers processing receipts independently.
tradeoffs: Breaks backwards compatibility for applications relying on cumulative gas or block-level log indexing.

scaleL1.description: Removing stateful receipt fields enables greater parallelism in block processing, supporting L1 throughput scaling.
